papers.summary:
Reward models are a key component of large language model alignment, serving as proxies for human preferences during training. However, existing evaluations focus primarily on broad instruction-following benchmarks, providing limited insight into whether these models capture socially desirable preferences. As a result, important failures in social alignment can remain hidden.

papers.summary_zh: 獎勵模型 (Reward models) 是大型語言模型 (LLM) 對齊 (alignment) 的關鍵組成部分，在訓練期間充當人類偏好 (human preferences) 的代理。然而，現有的評估主要集中在廣泛的指令遵循基準 (instruction-following benchmarks) 上，對於這些模型是否捕捉到社會期望的偏好 (socially desirable preferences) 提供的見解有限。因此，社會對齊 (social alignment) 中的重要失敗可能仍然隱藏。我們將獎勵模型基準測試 (reward-model benchmarking) 擴展到四個具有社會影響的領域：偏見 (bias)、安全性 (safety)、道德 (morality) 和倫理推理 (ethical reasoning)。

papers.summary: For Transformer models, cryptographically secure inference ensures that the client learns only the final output, while the server learns nothing about the client's input. primary_link.title: Average Attention Transformers and Arithmetic Circuits

top5.summary: We analyse the computational power of transformer encoders as sequence-to-sequence functions on vectors. We show that average hard attention can be used to simulate arithmetic circuits if they are given as an input to an encoder. primary_link.title: Superposition Is Not Necessary: A Mechanistic Interpretability Analysis of Transformer Representations for Time Series Forecasting

links.title: The Impossibility Triangle of Long-Context Modeling

primary_link.title: The Impossibility Triangle of Long-Context Modeling

top5.summary_zh: 我們識別並證明了一個支配 long-sequence models 的基本 trade-off：沒有任何模型可以同時實現 (i) 與 sequence length 無關的 per-step computation (Efficiency)，(ii) 與 sequence length 無關的 state size (Compactness)，以及 (iii) 回憶與 sequence length 成比例的 historical facts 的能力 (Recall)。我們在一個統一了 Transformers、state space models、linear recurrent networks、a 的 Online Sequence Processor 抽象框架內形式化了這個 trade-off
